location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week, including Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 6+ years leading enterprise technology program delivery

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years executing complex transformation programs in operations, consulting, strategy, or enterprise technology
6+ years leading enterprise technology program delivery, with demonstrated experience in ERP implementations (Oracle Financials) and post acquisition (M&A) technology integrations
Proven track record executing full-cycle transformations from business case development through implementation, change management, and operational handoff
Experience leading privacy and security programs such as GDPR/CCPA compliance, security framework implementations, identity management systems, or data governance initiatives
Experience leading enterprise-wide AI and automation initiatives by identifying high-impact generative AI opportunities to optimize processes and drive a culture of continuous operational excellence
Strong executive communication skills with ability to manage up to C-level stakeholders and translate complex technical and regulatory requirements into business impact
Demonstrated experience managing technology vendors, complex procurement processes, and success managing technology budgets exceeding $1M annually
Track record coordinating cross-functional teams across IT, Engineering, Security, Privacy, Compliance, Finance, HR, and business operations
Strong business acumen with the ability to identify strategic needs, lead all aspects of planning, and generate alignment with key business partners
Forward-thinking strategist with proven success in translating strategy to tactical plans with high-quality execution and delivery.
An empathetic leader style and a strong sense of self-awareness

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideal candidate also has experience supporting one of the following: HCM platforms (Workday, Greenhouse),  CRM rollouts (Salesforce), IT infrastructure and operations (network engineering, systems engineering, identity management, endpoint/MDM management, SaaS and collaboration platform migrations, and/or IT service management), security tool deployments, or privacy compliance initiatives

scope.surface: Corporate Engineering, IT, Privacy and Security — complex multi-year enterprise technology transformation programs
scope.business_model: internal corporate/enterprise IT function at a ride-sharing company
primary_metrics_inferred: Risk reduction across compliance and security programs
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end program delivery for complex, multi-year initiatives such as financial technology transformations (Oracle, Anaplan), people technology transformations (Workday, Greenhouse), sales technology rollouts (Salesforce), IT infrastructure transformations, privacy compliance programs (GDPR, CCPA), and security implementations
Execute strategic planning sessions and cross-functional prioritization with Corporate Engineering, IT, Privacy and Security leadership teams to align program roadmaps with business objectives
Manage program governance through biweekly and monthly strategic initiative reviews, risk assessments, and executive reporting that drives accountability and informed decision-making
Orchestrate cross-functional program teams spanning engineering, IT operations, security, privacy, compliance, and business stakeholders to ensure seamless program delivery
Drive enterprise technology lifecycle management including vendor selection, contract negotiations, system integrations, and sunset/migration planning
Proactively manage program risks and dependencies, implementing course corrections and executive stakeholder communications to keep initiatives on track
Partner with finance and strategy teams to manage program budgets, track ROI, and optimize resource allocation across the technology portfolio

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Hybrid schedule with specific days named — clear expectations
Scope of role is clearly defined with concrete technology programs named
Lyft commits to inclusive hiring and accommodation for persons with disabilities
18 weeks paid parental leave for biological, adoptive, and foster parents
Flexibility to work from anywhere up to 4 weeks per year
Existing vacancy — not a new headcount request
red_flags: Role is Toronto-based only with 3-day in-office requirement — limits remote candidates
Very broad scope spanning ERP, HCM, CRM, security, privacy, IT infra — may indicate under-resourcing or diffuse ownership
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Compensation stated in CAD only; no USD equivalent provided
AI screening disclosed ('Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants')

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required and comp range)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated; JD provides CAD figures only)
ambiguities: The 'empathetic leader style and strong sense of self-awareness' bullet appears in the Experience section without explicit 'required' or 'preferred' language — treated as hard given its placement alongside other unconditional requirements.
The AI/automation experience bullet is listed in the Experience section without 'preferred' qualifier — treated as hard but could be argued as soft given the broader list context.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are entirely absent from the JD — classified as 'unclear' for both fields.
